data.title: U.S. equities rally as Trump signals potential wind-down of Iran war
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages pushed higher on Wednesday as an end to the Iran conflict could be near, with President Donald Trump prepared to wind down the military campaign against the country within weeks.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +1.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, ten of the 11 S&P segments were in the green, with consumer discretionary at the top. At the other end of the spectrum, utilities have suffered the most. 

Treasury yields edged lower despite a jump in March across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dropped 5 basis points to 3.78%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) declined 2 basis points to 4.89%.

Trump also urged countries hit by Strait of Hormuz fuel disruptions to buy from the U.S. or “take it” themselves in remarks highlighting tensions with allies over the Iran war.

The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump told aides he is willing to end hostilities on a four-to-six-week timeline, prioritizing the degradation of Iran’s navy and missile stocks over the more complex mission of reopening the Strait of Hormuz. 

On the economic calendar, investors will soon see Chicago PMI and JOLTS data land. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Marvell Technology (MRVL) climbed +7.2%, while shares of Biogen (BIIB) dropped -4.3%.

data.title: Chicago PMI falls more than expected in March
data.description: The Chicago PMI fell to 52.8 in March from 57.7 in the prior month, against the 54.0 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Tuesday.

data.title: BofA highlights investor feedback on Microsoft, Coreweave on market share, valuation
data.description:
BofA highlighted investor feedback on Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), Coreweave (CRWV), and Nebius (NBIS) regarding market share, valuation, and risks, among other things.

Analysts led by Tal Liani said investors are increasingly focused on how market share dynamics evolve as adoption of agentic tools accelerates.

"For example, investors expressed skepticism around Microsoft’s positioning, noting slower-than-expected Copilot adoption. At the same time, we believe Microsoft’s unique combination of a broad enterprise software footprint and large-scale cloud infrastructure could provide meaningful leverage as agentic workloads begin attaching to existing applications," the analysts noted.

The analysts added that two themes consistently emerged around the Neoclouds: how to value them and can they sustain the value proposition in the long run.

The analysts believe these companies should ultimately be assessed on EV/EBIT or EV/EBITDA and plan to address this in the future. In addition, investors questioned the long-run viability of the Neocloud model beyond the current supply-constrained environment. If these companies remain purely overflow-capacity providers, they risk losing relevance once hyperscalers build sufficient in-house infrastructure, the analysts added.

"Nebius has a clear strategy with its early push into enterprise-focused software and tooling, while CoreWeave needs to sharpen its message on how it could sustain its technical leadership in the future, and focus on execution near term. Overall, long-term winners need to pair raw capacity with differentiated software, enterprise adoption, and value-add services that extend beyond infrastructure alone," said Liani and his team.

Microsoft (MSFT)

The analysts said that their discussion was centered around three key debates.

1. The risk of Microsoft’s share loss.

The analysts noted that a recurring theme was the skepticism around Microsoft’s AI leadership and the durability of its competitive positioning. Investors questioned the analysts' positive outlook amid the rapid scaling of OpenAI's (OPENAI) ChatGPT, Anthropic's (ANTHRO) Claude, Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini, and a growing field of open‑source models, which has translated to the risk of diminishing value for Microsoft’s Copilot. This skepticism has been reinforced by modest Copilot penetration of about 15M paid seats, or around 3.5% of paid M365 commercial seats, an underwhelming number given Microsoft’s extensive installed base, the analysts added.

"We believe this framing misses Microsoft’s core strategy of focusing on tightly bundled functionality rather than best‑of‑breed solutions. In cybersecurity for example, Microsoft is not the technical leader in any single segment, yet it has become the largest and fastest‑growing cybersecurity vendor. We see similar dynamics emerging in AI, where Microsoft’s control of enterprise workflows like M365, Dynamics, Security, identity and data gravity, creates meaningful advantages in agentic AI adoption. In this construct, Copilot will be a bundling driver of ARPU [average revenue per user] expansion, while the longer‑term upside is likely driven by usage‑based," said Liani and his team.

2. Azure constraint and capacity reallocation.

The analysts said last quarter Microsoft noted it is diverting compute for internal capacity instead offocusing on revenue-generating Azure. The company noted this last quarter that it would have grown over 40% year-over-year (in constant currency) but grew 38% year-over-year due to these internal capacity shifts.

"The reallocations could limit upside to growth expectations, and we are unsure of the rational given the company has means of offloading capacity to Neocloud players, bringing the questions of whether these capacity dynamics reflect genuine supply constraints or mask demand variability," the analysts noted.

3. OpenAI exposure.

The analysts said that another issue was the risk of OpenAI not being able to grow revenues as committed. Management noted that half of its infrastructure is used for its own internal activitiesand half to service others.

"Since the company works in an excess demand environment, any capacity shortfall of a single customer could be rerouted to others or to internal needs, although we believe that any shortfall from OAI will be a negative driver for the stocks of all Cloud companies," according to the analysts.

The analysts believe Microsoft's stock is attractive, and they see two main catalysts: orders converted to revenues as data center buildouts unlock some revenue backlog; and growth in demand for agentic AI, with Microsoft bundling agents with its own applications.

Oracle (ORCL)

"Oracle is a controversial stock with investors, and a few investors noted that while the long-term narrative is compelling, the near-term setup lacks catalysts. The prevailing view is that the RPO [Remaining Performance Obligations] build is impressive but revenue conversion remains largely backend weighted, over 3 years out. The primary gating factor is clear evidence that data center execution remains on track," said Liani and his team.

The analysts noted that the stock is down nearly 50% from the peak, and the risks are already reflected in the stock price and in street expectations. The analysts expect data center capacity to come online in the next few months, which should remove some of the execution concerns and help the stock.

Nebius (NBIS)

Liani and his team said that investor interest in Nebius was surprisingly significant, given the relatively small size of the company. The analysts highlight four key points of discussion: underappreciated value of non-core assets; execution risk on data center buildouts; enterprise penetration; and leverage and funding needs.

Coreweave (CRWV)

The analysts said the financing structure is not fully understood. They noted that a key discussion point was on the debt structure. 

In addition, the analysts noted that they had several discussions on the long-term sustainability of CoreWeave’s technical moat, especially once hyperscalers ramp up their own compute capacity.

data.title: Inspira Technologies terminates ATM, SEPA equity facilities
data.description:
• Inspira Technologies (Nasdaq: IINN) on Tuesday said that it has terminated its sales agreement with A.G.P./Alliance Global Partners and its standby equity purchase agreement with YA II PN Ltd.
  • The company said it has issued formal notices to end both financing arrangements, with no outstanding obligations or further advances to be made by either party.
  • Inspira said the move reflects its focus on maintaining a disciplined capital structure as it prepares for its next strategic phase.
  • IINN -2.03% premarket to $0.389.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Snowflake names Beaulier as Chief Revenue Officer, reaffirms guidance
data.description:
• Data warehousing company Snowflake (SNOW) said on Tuesday that it has named Jonathan Beaulier as its Chief Revenue Officer.
  • Shares fell 2% in premarket trading on the back of the news.
  • In his new role, Beaulier will be responsible for Snowflake’s revenue and go-to-market teams, Snowflake said in a statement. Beaulier will be replacing Mike Gannon as Snowflake's Chief Revenue Officer, who has decided to leave the company for personal reasons. Beaulier was previously Snowflake’s GVP, U.S. Majors Sales. He has served in other roles at the company as well.
  • “Having spent the last decade helping to build this organization, I am incredibly energized to take on this new role,” said Beaulier. “Our AI-first approach has opened up a unique opportunity for us to deliver even more value to our customers and I look forward to leveraging my experience with our products and customers to spearhead our next chapter of growth.”
  • In accordance with Beaulier's promotion, Snowflake reaffirms its guidance for the first quarter and full year of fiscal 2027. Snowflake said previously it expects product revenue between $1.262B and $1.267B for the first quarter and $5.66B in product revenue for the full year.

data.title: AleAnna reports Q4 results
data.description:
• AleAnna press release (ANNA): Q4
  • Revenue of $9.14M (+1087.0% Y/Y).
  • Net income of $0.3M

  • AleAnna closed the period with a strong cash position of $31.8 million, supporting ongoing development activity and future strategic initiatives.
